Title: MemorySmith.Agent — WebSocketBridge & World Adapter Transport
Content:
The WebSocket bridge is the transport layer between C# host and Node.js Mineflayer subprocess. It handles serialization, deserialization, reconnection, keep-alive, and error routing.

## Architecture

C# (WebUI.Blazor) → MinecraftAdapter → WebSocketBridge → WebSocket → Node.js (MineflayerAdapter/index.js) → Minecraft Server

## IWorldAdapter Interface (Agent.Core/Interfaces/IWorldAdapter.cs)

- ConnectAsync(CancellationToken) → spawns Node.js subprocess, connects WebSocket
- SendActionAsync(ActionData, CancellationToken) → sends JSON message
- ReceiveEventsAsync(CancellationToken) → returns IAsyncEnumerable<WorldEvent>
- DisconnectAsync() → clean shutdown

## MinecraftAdapter (Agent.World.Minecraft/MinecraftAdapter.cs)

- On ConnectAsync: locates Node.js executable, spawns index.js with configured env vars (SERVER_HOST, SERVER_PORT, BOT_USERNAME, WS_PORT)
- Waits for WebSocket connection with port polling and timeout
- On disconnect: retries unless cancellation requested

## WebSocketBridge (Agent.World.Minecraft/WebSocketBridge.cs)

### SendActionAsync

Serializes ActionData to JSON: {action, arguments, correlationId}. Sends via WebSocket.SendAsync. On send failure: writes to _messageChannel (for retry).

### ReceiveEventsAsync

Background receive loop reads from WebSocket, deserializes JSON, calls ParseEvent, yields parsed events via Channel<WorldEvent>. Channel provides backpressure — if C# can't keep up, messages buffer in the channel.

### ParseEvent (line ~210)

Reads root['event'] as string, pattern-matches to typed event constructor. Uses local helpers GetInt, GetString, GetDouble (~lines 385-410). Unknown event types return null and are silently dropped.

### Reconnection

- On disconnect: KickedEvent cancels _connectionCts → ExecuteAsync observes cancellation → reconnect loop
- Reconnect uses exponential backoff: [2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s] (DefaultReconnectDelays)
- _currentGoal is preserved across reconnections → agent resumes after reconnect

### Message Format

C# → Node.js:
{
  'action': 'mineBlock' | 'moveTo' | 'placeBlock' | 'craftItem' | 'getStatus' | 'chat' | 'wander' | 'findFlatArea' | 'findReachableBlock' | 'stop' | 'smeltItem',
  'arguments': { ... },
  'correlationId': 'guid'
}

Node.js → C#:
{
  'event': 'blockMined' | 'moveComplete' | 'chat' | 'status' | 'error' | ...,
  ...payload fields
}

## Key Constants (AGENTS.md convention)

- WS_PORT: default 3000 (configurable)
- DefaultReconnectDelays: [2000, 4000, 8000, 16000, 32000] ms
- MessageBufferSize: Channel capacity for event backpressure

## Error Handling

- Send failures: message written to _messageChannel for retry
- Receive failures: disconnected event → _connectionCts cancel → reconnect
- Malformed JSON: logged, message dropped
- Unknown event types: silently dropped (logged at debug level)
